Bitcoin has already won.  Clearer every day.

From holder's perspective, STRC is a guaranteed 11.5% based on a well-capitalized company bearing all the risk that Bitcoin will average higher CAGR.

Holding MSTR means assuming the BTC-per-share grows because fiat-based fixed income investors continue to want 11.5% and delta between cost of capital and Bitcoin CAGR accrues to the common stock holders.

text:
Conceptually, I still think P/BYD is key.  Hard part is to select for a period length that is representative of unknown rate of future BTC Yield.

Logically, with the "amplified BTC" model that Strategy has clearly articulated, it's easy to see how one might be willing to pay a premium for >1x BTC exposure (aka, Strategy has a 36% amplification ratio, meaning 1.36x BTC exposure).  

In turn, a willingness to pay a premium for that kind of exposure enables BTC Yield from new equity issuance, which is what P/BYD considers.

P/BYD explained: investors.smarterwebcompany.…

text:
BTCTCs continue to deliver BTC Yield when and how they can.

With @smarterwebuk warrant buybacks last week, SWC is off to a great start for Q2 in terms of the key BTCTC metric: growing Bitcoin ownership for shareholders.

In my view, there's a sweet spot where a BTCTC is de-risked enough in scale and positioning to execute well on the playbook, but small enough in terms of BTC holdings that it's easier to deliver larger BTC Yield numbers whenever good conditions arise.

Note these numbers are as of the weekend, and chart made by SWC shareholder @johnsthor1
